violations.text: The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (0) parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

(Chicharron and beef), which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of (PIC ), (Chicharron and beef) was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Sponges, gloves, etc were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing.PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (0) parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC added chlorine to proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

(chicharron), which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours.PIC voluntarily disposed of product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

(Various food product) was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of (PIC), (Chicharron) was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep.PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Dead rodent and fly observed on glue traps.PIC removed from premise. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
(Food product ) were observed stored on the floor in the (basement stairs ). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
Pans of cooked food product observed being stored/stacked on top of each other without being covered. PIC informed and covered the pans. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
(Chest freezers ) located in the (basement area ) area was not in good repair. Interior surface was damaged exposing insulation and metal. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

The cutting boards in the (kitchen and basement) are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (chlorine ) sanitizing solution for the (3 bay sink ). New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the (single service storage shelves in basement ) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

The sink used for food preparation and ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

The floors in basement have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and /or old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
